headline: Siemens Energy Q2 FY26: record €17.7B orders, raises FY guide to 14-16% revenue growth; accelerates €6B buyback
summary: Q2 orders hit all-time high €17.7B (book-to-bill 1.72) led by Gas Services and Grid Technologies. Revenue +8.9% YoY to €10.3B; profit doubled to €1.11B; net income €835m vs €501m. FCF pre-tax surged to €1.98B. Backlog €154B. FY26 outlook raised: revenue growth 14-16% (prior 12-14%), profit margin 10-12%, net income ~€4B, FCF pre-tax ~€8B. First €2B tranche of €6B buyback substantially complete (11.6M shares at avg €157.1); up to €1B more this FY. CEO Bruch flagged datacenter-driven demand running into the 2030s.

headline: Susquehanna lifts Nvidia PT to $275 from $250 ahead of May 20 print; raises Blackwell+Rubin '24-'27 rev to ~$1T
summary: Christopher Rolland reiterated Buy, citing accelerating Blackwell ramps and hyperscaler capex nearly doubling in 2026. Updated Blackwell + Rubin cumulative revenue through 2027 to ~$1T from $940B prior. Top-5 hyperscaler capex now expected at $725B for 2026 (+77% YoY). Sets up beat-and-raise expectations into the May 20 FY27 Q1 report.

headline: Citi raises Broadcom PT to $500 from $475; custom-silicon and AI networking thesis intact
summary: Citi lifted AVGO target to $500 (~26% upside from prior close ~$396) on continued hyperscaler ASIC pipeline strength and VMware integration ramp. Consensus PT $451; 28 analysts at Strong Buy. Setup follows AMD/MU rally driving 'changing of the guard' AI narrative into NVDA earnings next week.

headline: April CPI +0.6% MoM / +3.8% YoY hotter than expected on energy shock; chip rally stalls pre-market
summary: Headline CPI +0.6% MoM vs +0.5% expected; YoY 3.8%, highest since May 2023. Energy +3.8% MoM / +17.9% YoY drove >40% of the increase. Core +2.7% YoY, six-month high. Print pushes back rate-cut bets and pressures long-duration AI multiples; Nasdaq 100 futures -0.82%, S&P -0.38% pre-bell. AI-rally leaders (NVDA, AMD, MU, AVGO) all indicated lower into the open.

headline: Oklo reports Q1 after-close today; NRC Principal Design Criteria approval and Nvidia partnership underpin SMR thesis
summary: Street expects ~$(0.19) EPS loss. Catalysts: NRC approved Aurora PDC topical report May 6 (set safety/performance basis for all future Aurora variants); strategic AI-datacenter partnership with Nvidia disclosed earlier in May. Stock up >30% in past month into the print. Consensus PT $92.38, Moderate Buy (10 Buy / 5 Hold).

headline: Cisco Q3 FY26 prints tomorrow after-close; AI infra orders milestone and Silicon One G300 momentum in focus
summary: Guided rev $15.4-$15.6B, EPS $1.02-$1.04 (vs street $1.04 / $15.54B). AI infra orders ran $2.1B in Q2; FY26 AI-order target raised to >$5B with ~$3B converting to hyperscaler revenue. Street expects Q3 AI hyperscaler orders of ~$1.5B (vs conservative $1B bogey). Silicon One shipped its millionth chip; G300 targets 102.4 Tbps for hyperscalers/neoclouds. Splunk on pace to 1,000 new logos by year-end.

headline: GE Vernova Q1 datacenter orders hit $2.4B — already above all of 2025; 100 GW gas-turbine contracts on the books
summary: GEV signed 21 GW of new gas-turbine agreements in Q1, lifting cumulative GW under contract from 83 to 100; slot reservation agreements climbed from 43 to 56 GW. Roughly 20% explicitly tied to datacenter load; ~80% utility/IPP/industrial. Blue Energy + GEV separately announced a 2.5 GW gas-plus-nuclear hybrid project pairing BWRX-300 SMRs with gas turbines in Texas (first power ~2030).

headline: Goldman previews Nvidia May 20: expects beat-and-raise, lifts FY26/27 EPS 14%/34% above consensus; $1T DC TAM
summary: James Schneider expects management to reiterate mid-70% GM for CY26 and update the $1T cumulative datacenter revenue framework. Goldman raised revenue/EPS by ~12%; FY26 EPS now 14% above Street, FY27 34% above. Calls NVDA 'meaningfully undervalued' into the print with sequential upside from non-guided product lines (networking, Spectrum-X, sovereign).
